entry: this morning I was in the train and I kept thinking about finding an old photo and feeling the loss all over again. I tried to act normal, but it was like my face was doing one thing and the inside of me was doing something else. Daniel asked if I was okay and I said yes because explaining felt too big. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: A loss like this can keep echoing long after the moment has passed. The fact that this still hurts says something about what mattered, not something wrong with you. What part of this loss feels most present tonight?

entry: The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I answered messages, did the dishes, nodded at the right times, and still felt tender underneath all of it. I kept replaying little details and turning them into proof that something is wrong with me. I wish I could be more calm about it, but today I just couldn't get there. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: There is a lot of tenderness in what you wrote, and none of it sounds small. It makes sense that part of you wants to keep functioning while another part is still sitting with what changed. What do you wish someone understood about this without you having to explain it?

Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I answered messages, did the dishes, nodded at the right times, and still felt needy underneath all of it. I kept replaying little details and turning them into proof that something is wrong with me. I wish I could be more calm about it, but today I just couldn't get there.
reply: This sounds painful because the person matters to you. There may be a difference between what happened and the story your hurt is building around it. What would feeling cared for have looked like today?

entry: after the call I was in the parking lot and I kept thinking about feeling distant from family after a tense conversation. I tried to act normal, but it was like my face was doing one thing and the inside of me was doing something else. Leah asked if I was okay and I said yes because explaining felt too big. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: There is a lot of wanting-to-be-seen underneath this. A part of you seems to want repair, while another part is trying not to be hurt again. What did you most need from them in that moment?

entry: The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I answered messages, did the dishes, nodded at the right times, and still felt tender underneath all of it. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: The closeness and hurt seem tangled together here. Wanting closeness does not make you too much; it means the bond matters. What are you afraid would happen if you said the truest version gently?

entry: right before bed I was in the bathroom floor and I kept thinking about missing someone who used to be part of daily life. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: This sounds like a small light you actually allowed yourself to notice. Letting good things count can be its own kind of practice. What would it be like to let this count without explaining it away?

entry: after lunch I was in the train and I kept thinking about laughing with a friend after a hard week. I tried to act normal, but it was like my face was doing one thing and the inside of me was doing something else. Theo asked if I was okay and I said yes because explaining felt too big. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I answered messages, did the dishes, nodded at the right times, and still felt warm underneath all of it. I kept replaying little details and turning them into proof that something is wrong with me. I wish I could be more calm about it, but today I just couldn't get there. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: This has a warmth to it that feels worth letting in slowly. This moment seems to show that your effort is becoming visible in small, real ways. How can you remember this feeling when things get hard again?

Part of me wants to fix it immediately and part of me just wants someone to sit beside me and not make me justify why it matters.
reply: The closeness and hurt seem tangled together here. Wanting closeness does not make you too much; it means the bond matters. What are you afraid would happen if you said the truest version gently?

entry: on the way home I was in the coffee shop and I kept thinking about wondering if they are asking too much. I tried to act normal, but it was like my face was doing one thing and the inside of me was doing something else. Sam asked if I was okay and I said yes because explaining felt too big. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I answered messages, did the dishes, nodded at the right times, and still felt tender underneath all of it. I kept replaying little details and turning them into proof that something is wrong with me. I wish I could be more calm about it, but today I just couldn't get there.
reply: This sounds painful because the person matters to you. There may be a difference between what happened and the story your hurt is building around it. What would feeling cared for have looked like today?

entry: after the call I was in the train and I kept thinking about finding an old photo and feeling the loss all over again. I tried to act normal, but it was like my face was doing one thing and the inside of me was doing something else. Daniel asked if I was okay and I said yes because explaining felt too big. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: A loss like this can keep echoing long after the moment has passed. The fact that this still hurts says something about what mattered, not something wrong with you. What part of this loss feels most present tonight?

entry: this morning I was in the kitchen and I kept thinking about spiraling about a message that has not been answered. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. The weird part is that nothing looked huge from the outside. I answered messages, did the dishes, nodded at the right times, and still felt fed up underneath all of it. I kept replaying little details and turning them into proof that something is wrong with me. I wish I could be more calm about it, but today I just couldn't get there. I can see two sides at once, which makes it harder. One side of me knows this is probably just a hard day and not my whole life. The other side keeps whispering that this says something permanent about me or about how things are going to be. I am tired of having to talk myself down from my own thoughts.
reply: That frustration sounds like it has been building for longer than just today. It sounds like you have been carrying more than you wanted to admit. What would feel honest to admit before deciding what to do next?

entry: after lunch I was in the office and I kept thinking about being interrupted and dismissed again. I tried to act normal, but it was like my face was doing one thing and the inside of me was doing something else. Alex asked if I was okay and I said yes because explaining felt too big. Maybe that is dramatic, I don't know, but the feeling stayed with me for hours. Honestly I am embarrassed by how much this affected me, but pretending it didn't would be another kind of lie.
reply: Something in you seems to be saying, very clearly, that this was not okay. The sharpness may be protecting something vulnerable that did not feel heard. What boundary or need is this anger pointing toward?
